subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child understands that kidneys remove waste from the blood and turn it into urine, which is stored in the bladder.
Your child knows common acids like lemon juice and vinegar, and bases like baking soda and ammonia, and which ones can be harmful.
Your child knows common acids like vinegar and bases like ammonia and understands their dangers.
Your child can identify common acids like lemon juice and bases like baking soda and knows their hazards.
Your child learns to tell which common acids are safe and which can be harmful.
Your child knows which common acids are safe and which can be harmful.
Your child can check if security tools like passwords and virus protection are being used correctly to keep information safe.
Your child knows common acids like vinegar and bases like ammonia, and understands which ones can be dangerous.
canonical_name: Grade 8 Science: Acids and Bases — Common Examples and Safety
coherence_reason: The majority of standards focus on identifying common acids and bases and their safety, but one standard is about kidneys and another about cybersecurity, which are separate concepts.
split_topics: Acids and Bases — Common Examples and Safety
Human Body Systems — Kidneys and Waste Removal
Digital Security — Safe Use of Security Tools

subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child designs changes to computers to make them easier and better for people to use.
Your child can explain why computers can do some tasks more easily than people.
Digital tools
Your child knows what technology skills are needed for jobs.
Your child can name a technology made for space that we also use in everyday life, like computers or satellites.
Programming Applications
Your child can describe how computers use smart behaviors like moving robots, understanding speech, and recognizing images.
Your child can explain how people and machines work together to do tasks that neither can do alone.
canonical_name: Grade 8 Science: Technology and Computers — Human-Computer Interaction and Applications
coherence_reason: Most standards focus on how computers and technology interact with humans, their uses, and skills required, though there is some variation from skills to applications to collaboration.

subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child can join group discussions well and understand the information shared.
Knowledge and skills
Your child can identify important national people and celebrations that show values like fairness, justice, and helping others.
Your child earned credit in an elective class that encourages civic participation.
Your child understands what rights people have and what they should do as citizens in your community and state.
Career Research
Your child understands how different parts of the brain have special jobs and what happens if those parts get hurt.
Your child knows who the leaders are in your town and state and what they do.
canonical_name: Grade 8 Social Studies: Civics — Citizenship and Civic Participation
coherence_reason: The standards are poorly grouped, mixing citizenship, civic participation, neuroscience, career research, and group discussion skills.

subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child can explain, using evidence, that gravity pulls objects together and that this pull depends on how heavy the objects are.
Your child can make and use a model to show how a cell works and how its parts help it do its job.
Your child can make and share arguments to show that when an object's movement energy changes, energy moves to or from it.
Your child can do an investigation to show that all living things are made of one or more cells.
Your child can compare different design ideas carefully to see which one best meets the problem's needs and limits.
Your child can use code, pictures, or tools made by others in their own programs and give credit to the original creators.
Your child understands and can talk about how technology can be unfair or hard for some people to use.
Your child can describe the world using maps and other geography tools.
canonical_name: Grade 8 Science: Multiple Concepts — Gravity, Cells, Energy, Engineering Design, Technology, and Geography
coherence_reason: The standards cover several unrelated science and STEM topics, including gravity, cells, energy, engineering design, technology use, and geography, so they should be split into separate clusters.

subject: Science
sample_standards: Your child can design, build, test, and improve a device that either gives off or takes in heat through chemical reactions.
Your child can design, build, and test a device that either reduces or increases heat transfer.
Engineering Design
Energy
Developing Possible Solutions
PS1.B: Chemical Reactions
Your child uses science to design, build, and test a device that either reduces or increases heat transfer.
Definitions of Energy
canonical_name: Grade 8 Science: Energy — Heat Transfer and Engineering Design
coherence_reason: Most standards focus on designing devices involving heat transfer, with some specifically referencing chemical reactions, but all are under the umbrella of energy and engineering design.
